office_does: The auditor administers elections and vote-by-mail, maintains public records, manages licensing, and oversees county financial processes.
race_blurb: Jeremy Harrison-Smith emphasizes election access and administrative modernization; former councilmember Mark Hulst emphasizes management experience, legal compliance, and nonpartisan administration.

candidates.summary: Harrison-Smith is a regional government representative and former nonprofit leader running on election access and administrative modernization.
highlights: Supports vote-by-mail, expanded language access, and voter outreach [S1]
Calls for modern financial systems and improved licensing and records access [S1]

experience.basis: He brings relevant government and nonprofit administration but is a new candidate for the auditor's office.

candidates.summary: Hulst is a former Mount Vernon councilmember who emphasizes management experience, legal compliance, and politically independent administration.
highlights: Reports 12 years of city-council service, including finance and public-safety chair roles [S1]
Emphasizes accurate elections, transparent processes, trained staff, and strict legal compliance [S1]

experience.basis: Twelve years in elected city government and related board and management work give him an established public-sector record.
